real_insight: Recognize narrative scaffolding when you see it — not every meeting moment carries a teachable principle; some only mark a transition.
objection: The verse openly admits it is non-doctrinal and explicitly tells the reader that extracting a leadership lesson is a misread. That is a real meta-point about reading source material, not 'wisdom theater.' If the engineering layer had tried to extract 'always pause before you speak in incidents' as a doctrine, I'd have failed it on sight — and the layer specifically pre-empts that move in falsifiability. The insight is small but precise: the analog of the held-pause before the postmortem teaching begins. A senior engineer would recognize this — the Slack channel where everyone has stopped typing and nobody has yet spoken — as a real and named scene.
